subject: Add cpu&memory limitation
commit_message:
Add cpu&memory limitation

- Enable option to configure cpu&memory limit in common/postgres chart by using a resources from the postgres values.yaml file, not common.resources variable.
- Update vnfsdk charts to use cpu&memory limitation for Postgres HA database.

Issue-ID: VNFSDK-649
Signed-off-by: Zebek Bogumil <bogumil.zebek@nokia.com>
Change-Id: Ib0d8c13f0b6b54d543ef01b93ec491ec21ba0091

Patch Set 1:

or clearly describe wy it is not possible

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> or clearly describe wy it is not possible

Your common template does not work. 
In VNFSDK/values.yaml file there is a section postgres to override default configuration provided by common/postgres. It should be possible to override resources property too, but it doesn't work. It always uses empty resources value which is defined in common/postgres/values.yaml. My fix resolves the problem. I tested this correction at our lab.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > or clearly describe wy it is not possible
> 
> Your common template does not work. 
> In VNFSDK/values.yaml file there is a section postgres to override default configuration provided by common/postgres. It should be possible to override resources property too, but it doesn't work. It always uses empty resources value which is defined in common/postgres/values.yaml. My fix resolves the problem. I tested this correction at our lab.

Any more details what exactly doesn't work? Have you set .Values.global.flavor?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1:
> > > 
> > > or clearly describe wy it is not possible
> > 
> > Your common template does not work. 
> > In VNFSDK/values.yaml file there is a section postgres to override default configuration provided by common/postgres. It should be possible to override resources property too, but it doesn't work. It always uses empty resources value which is defined in common/postgres/values.yaml. My fix resolves the problem. I tested this correction at our lab.
> 
> Any more details what exactly doesn't work? Have you set .Values.global.flavor?

In vnfsdk/values.yaml there is a section:

# Resource Limit flavor -By Default using small
flavor: small
# Segregation for Different environment (Small and Large)
resources:
  small:
    limits:
      cpu: 2000m
      memory: 4Gi
    requests:
      cpu: 500m
      memory: 1Gi
  large:
    limits:
      cpu: 4000m
      memory: 8Gi
    requests:
      cpu: 1000m
      memory: 2Gi
  unlimited: {}

and it is used to configure limitation for vnfsdk. Additionally in mentioned values.yaml there is a section which is used to configure postgres:

# application configuration override for postgres
postgres:
  nameOverride: vnfsdk-postgres
  service:
    name: vnfsdk-dbset
    name2: vnfsdk-dbpri
    name3: vnfsdk-dbrep
  container:
    name:
      primary: vnfsdk-dbpri
      replica: vnfsdk-dbrep
  persistence:
    mountSubPath: vnfsdk/data
    mountInitPath: vnfsdk
  config:
    pgUserName: postgres
    pgDatabase: postgres
    pgUserExternalSecret: *pgUserCredsSecretName
    pgRootPasswordExternalSecret: *pgRootPassSecretName
  resources:
    limits:
      cpu: 4
      memory: 8Gi
    requests:
      cpu: 2
      memory: 2Gi

This postgres configuration should give us possibility to override resources configuration too.
From my point of view it is not acceptable to reuse resources parameter dedicated for vnfsdk component also for postgres. If we do this as you propose (but also your proposition does not work currently) we will have situation that some part of postgres configuration is set under postgres section and resources section is configured globally, once for vnfsdk and postgres.

I tested this correction at our lab.
> 
> Any more details what exactly doesn't work? Have you set .Values.global.flavor?

Also I tried as you proposed, but it does not work.

What I do:
1. I reverted changes in common/postgres, then execute: make common, next make onap -e SKIP_LINT=TRUE
2. I checked two configurations:
a. I put flavor to global section in vnfsdk/values.yaml

#################################################################
# Global configuration defaults.
#################################################################
global:
  nodePortPrefix: 302
  repository: nexus3.onap.org:10001
  readinessImage: onap/oom/readiness:3.0.1
  loggingRepository: docker.elastic.co
  loggingImage: beats/filebeat:5.5.0
  envsubstImage: dibi/envsubst
  flavor: small

and also I've added resources section under postgres section:

# application configuration override for postgres
postgres:
  nameOverride: vnfsdk-postgres
  service:
    name: vnfsdk-dbset
    name2: vnfsdk-dbpri
    name3: vnfsdk-dbrep
  container:
    name:
      primary: vnfsdk-dbpri
      replica: vnfsdk-dbrep
  persistence:
    mountSubPath: vnfsdk/data
    mountInitPath: vnfsdk
  config:
    pgUserName: postgres
    pgDatabase: postgres
    pgUserExternalSecret: *pgUserCredsSecretName
    pgRootPasswordExternalSecret: *pgRootPassSecretName
  resources:
    small:
      limits:
        cpu: 200m
        memory: 4Gi
      requests:
        cpu: 100m
        memory: 1Gi
    large:
      limits:
        cpu: 400m
        memory: 8Gi
      requests:
        cpu: 200m
        memory: 2Gi
    unlimited: {}

but it does not work. Nothing has happen. Default empty resources value has been taken from common/postgres/values.yaml

b. Next I tried to move flavor to postgres section:

# application configuration override for postgres
postgres:
  flavor: small
  nameOverride: vnfsdk-postgres
  service:
    name: vnfsdk-dbset
    name2: vnfsdk-dbpri
    name3: vnfsdk-dbrep
  container:
    name:
      primary: vnfsdk-dbpri
      replica: vnfsdk-dbrep
  persistence:
    mountSubPath: vnfsdk/data
    mountInitPath: vnfsdk
  config:
    pgUserName: postgres
    pgDatabase: postgres
    pgUserExternalSecret: *pgUserCredsSecretName
    pgRootPasswordExternalSecret: *pgRootPassSecretName
  resources:
    small:
      limits:
        cpu: 200m
        memory: 4Gi
      requests:
        cpu: 100m
        memory: 1Gi
    large:
      limits:
        cpu: 400m
        memory: 8Gi
      requests:
        cpu: 200m
        memory: 2Gi
    unlimited: {}

but also does not work.

Of course, after changes in vnfsdk charts I executed make vnfsdk, next make onap -e SKIP_LINT=TRUE, before installation.

Everything works as expected for me after you add resources definition as above. The flavor is not applied because it is overriden from onap chart in global values.

We have a standard precedence of values:
.Values.flavor
.Values.global.flavor
.Values.flavorOverride
